1→import 'dart:async';
     2→
     3→/// Network connection type
     4→enum NetworkConnectionType {
     5→  /// No network connectivity
     6→  none,
     7→
     8→  /// Device is not connected to any network
     9→  disconnected,
    10→
    11→  /// Ethernet/Wired connection
    12→  ethernet,
    13→
    14→  /// WiFi connection
    15→  wifi,
    16→
    17→  /// Mobile/cellular connection (2G, 3G, 4G, 5G)
    18→  mobile,
    19→
    20→  /// Bluetooth tethering
    21→  bluetooth,
    22→
    23→  /// VPN connection
    24→  vpn,
    25→
    26→  /// Other connection type
    27→  other,
    28→}
    29→
    30→/// Network status information
    31→class NetworkStatus {
    32→  /// Whether device is currently online
    33→  final bool isOnline;
    34→
    35→  /// Type of network connection
    36→  final NetworkConnectionType connectionType;
    37→
    38→  /// Timestamp when this status was detected
    39→  final DateTime timestamp;
    40→
    41→  const NetworkStatus({
    42→    required this.isOnline,
    43→    required this.connectionType,
    44→    required this.timestamp,
    45→  });
    46→
    47→  /// Creates an offline status
    48→  factory NetworkStatus.offline() {
    49→    return NetworkStatus(
    50→      isOnline: false,
    51→      connectionType: NetworkConnectionType.none,
    52→      timestamp: DateTime.now(),
    53→    );
    54→  }
    55→
    56→  /// Creates an online status with the given connection type
    57→  factory NetworkStatus.online(NetworkConnectionType connectionType) {
    58→    return NetworkStatus(
    59→      isOnline: true,
    60→      connectionType: connectionType,
    61→      timestamp: DateTime.now(),
    62→    );
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  /// Copy with method for immutability
    66→  NetworkStatus copyWith({
    67→    bool? isOnline,
    68→    NetworkConnectionType? connectionType,
    69→    DateTime? timestamp,
    70→  }) {
    71→    return NetworkStatus(
    72→      isOnline: isOnline ?? this.isOnline,
    73→      connectionType: connectionType ?? this.connectionType,
    74→      timestamp: timestamp ?? this.timestamp,
    75→    );
    76→  }
    77→
    78→  @override
    79→  String toString() =>
    80→      'NetworkStatus(isOnline: $isOnline, connectionType: $connectionType, '
    81→      'timestamp: $timestamp)';
    82→
    83→  @override
    84→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
    85→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
    86→
    87→    return other is NetworkStatus &&
    88→        other.isOnline == isOnline &&
    89→        other.connectionType == connectionType &&
    90→        other.timestamp == timestamp;
    91→  }
    92→
    93→  @override
    94→  int get hashCode => isOnline.hashCode ^ connectionType.hashCode ^ timestamp.hashCode;
    95→}
    96→
    97→/// Abstract interface for monitoring network connectivity
    98→///
    99→/// Implementations should:
   100→/// - Monitor network state changes in real-time
   101→/// - Provide streams for reactive updates
   102→/// - Handle network type transitions
   103→/// - Detect offline/online status changes
   104→abstract class NetworkConnectivity {
   105→  /// Current network status
   106→  NetworkStatus get currentStatus;
   107→
   108→  /// Stream of network status changes
   109→  ///
   110→  /// Emits a new [NetworkStatus] whenever the network state changes.
   111→  Stream<NetworkStatus> get statusStream;
   112→
   113→  /// Stream that emits [true] when device comes online
   114→  ///
   115→  /// Only emits when transitioning from offline to online.
   116→  Stream<bool> get onOnline;
   117→
   118→  /// Stream that emits [true] when device goes offline
   119→  ///
   120→  /// Only emits when transitioning from online to offline.
   121→  Stream<bool> get onOffline;
   122→
   123→  /// Check if device is currently online
   124→  ///
   125→  /// Returns [true] if device has network connectivity
   126→  bool get isOnline;
   127→
   128→  /// Get the current network connection type
   129→  ///
   130→  /// Returns [NetworkConnectionType.none] if device is offline
   131→  NetworkConnectionType get connectionType;
   132→
   133→  /// Initialize the connectivity monitor
   134→  ///
   135→  /// Should be called before using the service to ensure
   136→  /// initial network status is captured.
   137→  Future<void> initialize();
   138→
   139→  /// Start monitoring network status
   140→  ///
   141→  /// Begins listening for network changes and updating streams.
   142→  Future<void> startMonitoring();
   143→
   144→  /// Stop monitoring network status
   145→  ///
   146→  /// Stops listening for network changes and closes streams.
   147→  Future<void> stopMonitoring();
   148→
   149→  /// Dispose of resources
   150→  ///
   151→  /// Equivalent to calling [stopMonitoring].
   152→  void dispose();
   153→}
   154→
   155→/// Provider signature for dependency injection
   156→typedef NetworkConnectivityProvider = NetworkConnectivity Function();
   157→

entries.content:
Perfect! I've successfully completed **Subtask 2.1: Implement conflict detection logic**. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### Files Created (1,722 lines of code)

**Domain Models:**
- `EntityVersion` (164 lines) - Version tracking with numbers, timestamps, device IDs, and content hashes
- `ConflictInfo` (253 lines) - 5 conflict types and 3 severity levels with detailed information

**Domain Services:**
- `ConflictDetector` interface (192 lines) - 4 detection strategies (version-based, timestamp-based, hybrid, content-based)

**Infrastructure:**
- `ConflictDetectorImpl` (443 lines) - Hybrid strategy implementation combining version numbers, timestamps, and SHA256 content hashing

**Tests:**
- `entity_version_test.dart` (172 lines) - 10+ tests
- `conflict_detector_impl_test.dart` (498 lines) - 40+ tests covering all scenarios

### Key Features Implemented

✅ **Version Comparison Logic**
- Monotonic version number tracking
- Timestamp-based comparison
- Content hash comparison using SHA256

✅ **Conflict Detection**
- Same version with different content (concurrent edits)
- Diverged versions (different ancestors)
- Local newer / Remote newer scenarios
- Timestamp conflicts with configurable threshold (1s default)

✅ **False Positive Minimization**
- Hybrid strategy requiring both version AND timestamp agreement
- Configurable concurrent threshold
- Content hashing to detect actual changes
- Severity-based classification (auto-resolvable vs manual)

### All Acceptance Criteria Met

✅ **AC1:** Version comparison logic implemented  
✅ **AC2:** Conflicts detected when local and remote versions diverged  
✅ **AC3:** False positives minimized

### What's Next?

The implementation is ready for integration with the SyncService. The next subtasks are:
- 2.2: Create conflict resolution strategies
- 2.3: Build conflict resolution UI components  
- 2.4: Implement user decision handling

All code has been committed to git with a descriptive commit message following the project's conventions.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

entries.content: Excellent! Now let me update the implementation plan to mark this subtask as completed:

entries.detail:
1→{
     2→  "feature": "Sync & Conflict Resolution System",
     3→  "description": "# Sync & Conflict Resolution System\n\nRobust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet\n- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe\n- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available\n- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI\n- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff\n- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed\n- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)\n- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts\n- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep\n- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages\n",
     4→  "created_at": "2026-01-04T18:29:49.117Z",

Created SyncEntityType enum defining 9 syncable entity types (authTokens, profile, trip, tripPlanning, travelNote, locationUpdate, travelPreference, companions, sharedTrips) with priorities, offline support, and conflict resolution strategies. Used Riverpod for state management (not Redux/Zustand as those are React-specific). Added comprehensive SYNC_ARCHITECTURE.md documentation (12KB). All acceptance criteria met."

entries.content:
Perfect! I've successfully completed **Subtask 2.2: Create conflict resolution strategies**. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### What Was Built

**1. Domain Models (407 lines)**
- `ConflictResolution` - Tracks resolution strategy, data, and metadata
- `MergeResult` - Results of automatic merge operations
- `BatchResolutionResult` - Batch resolution tracking
- `ConflictResolutionStrategy` enum - 3 strategies
- `ManualResolutionChoice` enum - 3 user choice options

**2. Domain Service Interface (194 lines)**
- `ConflictResolver` abstract interface with 12 methods
- Configuration support with protected fields
- Smart strategy recommendation
- Comprehensive merge attempt capabilities

**3. Infrastructure Implementation (570 lines)**
- **Last-Write-Wins Strategy**: Timestamp-based selection with configurable preference
- **Manual Resolution Strategy**: Keep local, keep remote, or custom merge modes
- **Automatic Merge Strategy**: Merges non-overlapping fields with conflict tracking
- **Smart Strategy Recommendation**: Analyzes conflict type, severity, and data overlap
- **Protected Field Support**: Prevents auto-merge on critical data conflicts
- **Batch Resolution**: Handles multiple conflicts with error tracking

### Test Coverage (1,207 lines)
- **Model Tests**: 15+ test cases
- **Implementation Tests**: 30+ test cases
- Coverage includes: All strategies, merge scenarios, edge cases, batch resolution

### Key Features
✅ Last-write-wins with timestamp comparison  
✅ Manual resolution with 3 user choice modes  
✅ Automatic merge for compatible changes  
✅ Strategy selection based on conflict type and severity  
✅ Protected field support for sensitive data  
✅ Field-level tracking for merge transparency  
✅ User-friendly resolution descriptions  

## Acceptance Criteria - All Met ✅
- ✅ Last-write-wins strategy implemented
- ✅ Manual resolution strategy implemented  
- ✅ Automatic merge for compatible changes
- ✅ Strategy selection based on conflict type

**Total Implementation: 2,378 lines of production-ready code**

The implementation is fully committed and documented. Ready to move on to the next subtask!

I'll help you create a modal/dialog for showing both versions of conflicted data with side-by-side comparison and user choice controls. Let me start by understanding the context and existing code patterns.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

Let me look at existing UI patterns and the conflict resolution models:

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→import 'entity_version.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Type of conflict detected during sync
     5→enum ConflictType {
     6→  /// Both local and remote versions have the same version number
     7→  /// but different content (concurrent modification)
     8→  versionConflict,
     9→
    10→  /// Local version is newer than remote, but content differs
    11→  /// (remote has stale data)
    12→  localNewer,
    13→
    14→  /// Remote version is newer than local, but content differs
    15→  /// (local has stale data)
    16→  remoteNewer,
    17→
    18→  /// Versions are based on different ancestors (diverged history)
    19→  diverged,
    20→
    21→  /// Conflict detected via timestamp comparison
    22→  timestampConflict,
    23→}
    24→
    25→/// Severity level of a conflict
    26→enum ConflictSeverity {
    27→  /// Low severity - can be auto-resolved
    28→  low,
    29→
    30→  /// Medium severity - may require user input
    31→  medium,
    32→
    33→  /// High severity - requires manual resolution
    34→  high,
    35→}
    36→
    37→/// Detailed information about a detected conflict
    38→class ConflictInfo extends Equatable {
    39→  /// Unique identifier for this conflict
    40→  final String conflictId;
    41→
    42→  /// ID of the entity in conflict
    43→  final String entityId;
    44→
    45→  /// Type of entity
    46→  final String entityType;
    47→
    48→  /// Type of conflict detected
    49→  final ConflictType conflictType;
    50→
    51→  /// Severity of the conflict
    52→  final ConflictSeverity severity;
    53→
    54→  /// Local entity version
    55→  final EntityVersion localVersion;
    56→
    57→  /// Remote entity version
    58→  final EntityVersion remoteVersion;
    59→
    60→  /// Local entity data (if available)
    61→  final Map<String, dynamic>? localData;
    62→
    63→  /// Remote entity data (if available)
    64→  final Map<String, dynamic>? remoteData;
    65→
    66→  /// Human-readable description of the conflict
    67→  final String description;
    68→
    69→  /// When the conflict was detected
    70→  final DateTime detectedAt;
    71→
    72→  const ConflictInfo({
    73→    required this.conflictId,
    74→    required this.entityId,
    75→    required this.entityType,
    76→    required this.conflictType,
    77→    required this.severity,
    78→    required this.localVersion,
    79→    required this.remoteVersion,
    80→    this.localData,
    81→    this.remoteData,
    82→    required this.description,
    83→    required this.detectedAt,
    84→  });
    85→
    86→  /// Creates a copy with the given fields replaced
    87→  ConflictInfo copyWith({
    88→    String? conflictId,
    89→    String? entityId,
    90→    String? entityType,
    91→    ConflictType? conflictType,
    92→    ConflictSeverity? severity,
    93→    EntityVersion? localVersion,
    94→    EntityVersion? remoteVersion,
    95→    Map<String, dynamic>? localData,
    96→    Map<String, dynamic>? remoteData,
    97→    String? description,
    98→    DateTime? detectedAt,
    99→  }) {
   100→    return ConflictInfo(
   101→      conflictId: conflictId ?? this.conflictId,
   102→      entityId: entityId ?? this.entityId,
   103→      entityType: entityType ?? this.entityType,
   104→      conflictType: conflictType ?? this.conflictType,
   105→      severity: severity ?? this.severity,
   106→      localVersion: localVersion ?? this.localVersion,
   107→      remoteVersion: remoteVersion ?? this.remoteVersion,
   108→      localData: localData ?? this.localData,
   109→      remoteData: remoteData ?? this.remoteData,
   110→      description: description ?? this.description,
   111→      detectedAt: detectedAt ?? this.detectedAt,
   112→    );
   113→  }
   114→
   115→  /// Whether this conflict can be automatically resolved
   116→  bool get canAutoResolve {
   117→    return severity == ConflictSeverity.low ||
   118→        conflictType == ConflictType.localNewer ||
   119→        conflictType == ConflictType.remoteNewer;
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  /// Time since conflict was detected
   123→  Duration get age => DateTime.now().toUtc().difference(detectedAt);
   124→
   125→  @override
   126→  List<Object?> get props => [
   127→        conflictId,
   128→        entityId,
   129→        entityType,
   130→        conflictType,
   131→        severity,
   132→        localVersion,
   133→        remoteVersion,
   134→        localData,
   135→        remoteData,
   136→        description,
   137→        detectedAt,
   138→      ];
   139→
   140→  @override
   141→  String toString() =>
   142→      'ConflictInfo(conflictId: $conflictId, entityId: $entityId, '
   143→      'entityType: $entityType, conflictType: $conflictType, '
   144→      'severity: $severity, detectedAt: $detectedAt)';
   145→
   146→  /// Convert to JSON
   147→  Map<String, dynamic> toJson() {
   148→    return {
   149→      'conflictId': conflictId,
   150→      'entityId': entityId,
   151→      'entityType': entityType,
   152→      'conflictType': conflictType.name,
   153→      'severity': severity.name,
   154→      'localVersion': localVersion.toJson(),
   155→      'remoteVersion': remoteVersion.toJson(),
   156→      'localData': localData,
   157→      'remoteData': remoteData,
   158→      'description': description,
   159→      'detectedAt': detectedAt.toIso8601String(),
   160→    };
   161→  }
   162→
   163→  /// Create from JSON
   164→  factory ConflictInfo.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) {
   165→    return ConflictInfo(
   166→      conflictId: json['conflictId'] as String,
   167→      entityId: json['entityId'] as String,
   168→      entityType: json['entityType'] as String,
   169→      conflictType: ConflictType.values
   170→          .firstWhere((e) => e.name == json['conflictType']),
   171→      severity: ConflictSeverity.values
   172→          .firstWhere((e) => e.name == json['severity']),
   173→      localVersion: EntityVersion.fromJson(json['localVersion']),
   174→      remoteVersion: EntityVersion.fromJson(json['remoteVersion']),
   175→      localData: json['localData'] as Map<String, dynamic>?,
   176→      remoteData: json['remoteData'] as Map<String, dynamic>?,
   177→      description: json['description'] as String,
   178→      detectedAt: DateTime.parse(json['detectedAt'] as String),
   179→    );
   180→  }
   181→}
   182→
   183→/// Result of a conflict detection operation
   184→class ConflictDetectionResult extends Equatable {
   185→  /// Whether any conflicts were detected
   186→  final bool hasConflicts;
   187→
   188→  /// List of detected conflicts
   189→  final List<ConflictInfo> conflicts;
   190→
   191→  /// Total number of entities checked
   192→  final int entitiesChecked;
   193→
   194→  /// Number of entities without conflicts
   195→  final int noConflictCount;
   196→
   197→  const ConflictDetectionResult({
   198→    required this.hasConflicts,
   199→    required this.conflicts,
   200→    required this.entitiesChecked,
   201→    required this.noConflictCount,
   202→  });
   203→
   204→  /// Creates a result with no conflicts
   205→  factory ConflictDetectionResult.noConflict(int entitiesChecked) {
   206→    return ConflictDetectionResult(
   207→      hasConflicts: false,
   208→      conflicts: const [],
   209→      entitiesChecked: entitiesChecked,
   210→      noConflictCount: entitiesChecked,
   211→    );
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  /// Creates a result with conflicts
   215→  factory ConflictDetectionResult.withConflicts({
   216→    required List<ConflictInfo> conflicts,
   217→    required int entitiesChecked,
   218→  }) {
   219→    return ConflictDetectionResult(
   220→      hasConflicts: true,
   221→      conflicts: conflicts,
   222→      entitiesChecked: entitiesChecked,
   223→      noConflictCount: entitiesChecked - conflicts.length,
   224→    );
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  /// Number of conflicts detected
   228→  int get conflictCount => conflicts.length;
   229→
   230→  /// Conflicts by severity
   231→  Map<ConflictSeverity, List<ConflictInfo>> get conflictsBySeverity {
   232→    final map = <ConflictSeverity, List<ConflictInfo>>{};
   233→    for (final conflict in conflicts) {
   234→      map.putIfAbsent(conflict.severity, () => []).add(conflict);
   235→    }
   236→    return map;
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  /// High severity conflicts
   240→  List<ConflictInfo> get highSeverityConflicts =>
   241→      conflicts.where((c) => c.severity == ConflictSeverity.high).toList();
   242→
   243→  /// Medium severity conflicts
   244→  List<ConflictInfo> get mediumSeverityConflicts =>
   245→      conflicts.where((c) => c.severity == ConflictSeverity.medium).toList();
   246→
   247→  /// Low severity conflicts (auto-resolvable)
   248→  List<ConflictInfo> get lowSeverityConflicts =>
   249→      conflicts.where((c) => c.severity == ConflictSeverity.low).toList();
   250→
   251→  @override
   252→  List<Object?> get props => [hasConflicts, conflicts, entitiesChecked, noConflictCount];
   253→
   254→  @override
   255→  String toString() =>
   256→      'ConflictDetectionResult(hasConflicts: $hasConflicts, '
   257→      'conflictCount: $conflictCount, entitiesChecked: $entitiesChecked)';
   258→}
   259→

entries.content:
## Summary

I've successfully completed **Subtask 2.3 - Build Conflict Resolution UI Components** for the sync conflict resolution system. Here's what was implemented:

### 🎨 Widgets Created (2,270+ lines)

1. **ConflictResolutionDialog** - Modal dialog with:
   - Side-by-side version comparison
   - Visual severity indicators (orange/deep orange/red)
   - Three resolution options (Keep Local, Keep Remote, Merge)
   - Responsive design (side-by-side on ≥500px, stacked on mobile)
   - Entity information display

2. **ConflictComparisonView** - Side-by-side comparison with:
   - Color-coded version cards (primary/secondary)
   - Version metadata (numbers, relative timestamps, device IDs, hashes)
   - Data field comparison (up to 10 fields with truncation)

3. **ConflictListView** - Scrollable list with:
   - Card-based layout with severity styling
   - Quick summary with timestamps
   - Empty state handling
   - Auto-resolve support

4. **ConflictBanner** - Alert banners for:
   - Single conflicts
   - Multiple conflicts with severity breakdown
   - Gradient backgrounds and dismissible actions

### ✅ All Acceptance Criteria Met

✓ Conflict modal component created  
✓ Side-by-side diff display  
✓ Keep local/keep remote/merge options available  
✓ Mobile-responsive design  

### 📦 Deliverables

- **9 widget files** (4 main widgets + supporting files)
- **2 test files** (15+ test cases)
- **Comprehensive documentation** (README with usage examples)
- **Integration examples** (complete workflow demonstrations)

### 🚀 Quality Features

- Material 3 design with proper theming
- Responsive layout with automatic breakpoints
- Accessibility support (proper contrast, semantic labels)
- Performance optimized (efficient lists, value truncation)
- Error handling (null checks, optional parameters)

### 📝 Commit

**Commit**: `b313816` - "auto-claude: Subtask 2.3 - Build conflict resolution UI components"  
**Files**: 13 files changed, 3,418 insertions, 9 deletions

All files have been committed, the implementation plan has been updated, and the build progress has been documented. The implementation is ready for **Subtask 2.4 - Implement user decision handling**.
I'll help you implement this subtask. Let me start by reading the specification and implementation plan to understand the context.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

entries.content:
## Implementation Complete! ✨

I've successfully implemented **Subtask 2.4: Implement user decision handling** for the conflict resolution system. Here's what was delivered:

### 📁 Files Created

**Presentation Layer:**
1. **`conflict_resolution_state.dart`** (195 lines)
   - State model with pending conflicts, active conflict, resolution tracking
   - Factory constructors for all state scenarios
   - Computed properties for UI queries

2. **`conflict_resolution_notifier.dart`** (485 lines)
   - StateNotifier managing the complete resolution workflow
   - `applyUserChoice()` processes keepLocal/keepRemote/customMerge
   - `_applyResolutionToLocal()` applies resolution to data store
   - `_queueResolutionOperation()` queues sync operations
   - Batch resolution and auto-resolution support
   - Comprehensive logging and error handling

3. **`conflict_resolution_providers.dart`** (235 lines)
   - 10+ Riverpod providers for reactive integration
   - `ConflictResolutionStatus` enum with 6 states
   - Providers for counts, active conflict, resolution result

4. **`conflict_resolution_integration_example.dart`** (510 lines)
   - 4 complete integration scenarios
   - Real-world `SyncFlowExample` with `handleDetectedConflicts()`
   - State change listener examples

**Test File:**
5. **`conflict_resolution_notifier_test.dart`** (325 lines)
   - 15+ test cases covering all resolution workflows
   - State transitions, user choices, errors, batch resolution

### ✅ Acceptance Criteria Met

- ✓ **User choice applied to data store** - Via `_applyResolutionToLocal()`
- ✓ **Resolution operation queued for sync** - Via `_queueResolutionOperation()`  
- ✓ **UI updates after resolution** - Via AsyncValue state management

### 🔄 Integration Flow

```
User Choice (UI) → ConflictResolutionNotifier
                     ↓
               ConflictResolver
                     ↓
         ┌──────────┴──────────┐
         ↓                     ↓
   Local Data Store      SyncService Queue
   (apply resolution)    (queue for remote)
         ↓                     ↓
         └───────┬─────────────┘
                 ↓
         UI Updates (AsyncValue)
```

### 📦 Total Implementation

**1,250+ lines of code:**
- State model: 195 lines
- Notifier: 485 lines  
- Providers: 235 lines
- Integration example: 510 lines
- Tests: 325 lines

### 📝 Documentation Updated

- `implementation_plan.json` - Subtask 2.4 marked as completed
- `build-progress.txt` - Added detailed implementation notes

### 🎯 Next Steps

Phase 2 is now **complete**! Ready to move to **Phase 3: Retry & Error Handling**
- Subtask 3.1 - Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism

---

**Git commit:** `f3adbbe` - "auto-claude: Implement user decision handling for conflict resolution"

The implementation follows all existing code patterns (Riverpod state management, AsyncValue pattern, comprehensive logging) and includes full test coverage! 🚀
I'll help you implement retry logic with exponential backoff for failed sync operations. Let me start by reading the spec and understanding the context.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

    25→  - Configurable max delay (default: 60000ms = 60s)
    26→  - Jitter support to prevent thundering herd problem (default: ±10%)

    29→    - Retry 0: 1s (1000ms)
    30→    - Retry 1: 2s (2000ms)
    31→    - Retry 2: 4s (4000ms)
    32→    - Retry 3: 8s (8000ms)
    33→    - Retry 4: 16s (16000ms)
    34→    - Retry 5: 32s (32000ms)
    35→    - Retry 6+: 60s (60000ms, capped at max)
    36→  - calculateNextRetryTime(int retryCount, {DateTime? from}): Calculates DateTime of next retry
    37→  - calculateRemainingDelay(int retryCount, DateTime lastAttemptAt): Calculates remaining Duration
    38→  - getDelayDescription(int retryCount): Human-readable description for logging
    39→  - getDelaysForAttempts(int maxAttempts): Get all delay values up to max attempts
    40→  - Comprehensive validation with assertions

entries.content:
Perfect! I've successfully implemented subtask 3.1 - "Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism". Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### **What Was Built:**

1. **ExponentialBackoff Domain Service** (220+ lines)
   - Calculates exponential backoff delays: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 32s → 60s (max)
   - Configurable base delay (default: 1000ms) and max delay (default: 60000ms)
   - Jitter support (±10%) to prevent thundering herd problem
   - Three predefined strategies: standard, aggressive, conservative
   - Methods for calculating delays, next retry times, and remaining delays

2. **SyncOperation Model Updates**
   - Added `nextRetryAt` field to track when operation should be retried
   - Updated `shouldRetry()` to accept configurable max attempts
   - Added `isReadyForRetry` getter to check if operation can be processed
   - Added `timeUntilRetry` getter to calculate remaining wait time
   - Full JSON serialization support for retry fields

3. **SyncServiceImpl Integration**
   - Integrated ExponentialBackoff with configurable parameter
   - Uses `config.maxRetryAttempts` instead of hardcoded value (default: 5)
   - Calculates `nextRetryAt` using exponential backoff when retrying
   - Skips operations not ready for retry during processing
   - Schedules future retry processing with appropriate delays
   - Improved error logging with retry counts

4. **Comprehensive Test Coverage** (550+ lines)
   - **exponential_backoff_test.dart** (20+ test cases)
     - Delay calculations, custom configurations, jitter behavior
     - Time calculations, predefined strategies, validation
   - **sync_operation_retry_test.dart** (15+ test cases)
     - Retry logic, readiness checks, time calculations
     - JSON serialization, equality, edge cases

### **Acceptance Criteria - All Met:**
✅ Retry logic with exponential backoff implemented (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s)
✅ Max retry limit enforced (configurable, default 5 attempts)
✅ Backoff reset after successful sync (successful ops not re-queued)

### **Commit:**
```
38daaca - auto-claude: Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism
```

All files have been committed, and the implementation plan has been updated to mark subtask 3.1 as completed. The system is now ready for the next subtask: **3.2 - Add error categorization and messaging**.
I'll help you implement the error categorization system. Let me start by reading the spec and understanding the current state.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→import '../entities/sync_entity_type.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Represents the type of sync operation
     5→enum SyncOperationType {
     6→  /// Create a new entity
     7→  create,
     8→
     9→  /// Update an existing entity
    10→  update,
    11→
    12→  /// Delete an entity
    13→  delete,
    14→
    15→  /// Batch operation (multiple operations)
    16→  batch,
    17→}
    18→
    19→/// Represents a single sync operation in the queue
    20→class SyncOperation extends Equatable {
    21→  /// Unique identifier for this operation
    22→  final String id;
    23→
    24→  /// Type of entity to sync
    25→  final SyncEntityType entityType;
    26→
    27→  /// Operation type (create, update, delete, batch)
    28→  final SyncOperationType operationType;
    29→
    30→  /// Entity ID (for update/delete operations)
    31→  final String? entityId;
    32→
    33→  /// Operation payload (data to sync)
    34→  final Map<String, dynamic>? data;
    35→
    36→  /// Timestamp when operation was created
    37→  final DateTime createdAt;
    38→
    39→  /// Number of retry attempts
    40→  final int retryCount;
    41→
    42→  /// When this operation should be retried (null if not failed yet)
    43→  final DateTime? nextRetryAt;
    44→
    45→  /// Priority for ordering (higher = processed first)
    46→  final int priority;
    47→
    48→  /// Whether this operation can be batched with others
    49→  final bool canBatch;
    50→
    51→  /// ID of the batch this operation belongs to (if batched)
    52→  final String? batchId;
    53→
    54→  /// Version of the entity being synced
    55→  final int? version;
    56→
    57→  const SyncOperation({
    58→    required this.id,
    59→    required this.entityType,
    60→    required this.operationType,
    61→    this.entityId,
    62→    this.data,
    63→    required this.createdAt,
    64→    this.retryCount = 0,
    65→    this.nextRetryAt,
    66→    this.priority = 0,
    67→    this.canBatch = false,
    68→    this.batchId,
    69→    this.version,
    70→  });
    71→
    72→  /// Creates a copy of this operation with the given fields replaced
    73→  SyncOperation copyWith({
    74→    String? id,
    75→    SyncEntityType? entityType,
    76→    SyncOperationType? operationType,
    77→    String? entityId,
    78→    Map<String, dynamic>? data,
    79→    DateTime? createdAt,
    80→    int? retryCount,
    81→    DateTime? nextRetryAt,
    82→    int? priority,
    83→    bool? canBatch,
    84→    String? batchId,
    85→    int? version,
    86→  }) {
    87→    return SyncOperation(
    88→      id: id ?? this.id,
    89→      entityType: entityType ?? this.entityType,
    90→      operationType: operationType ?? this.operationType,
    91→      entityId: entityId ?? this.entityId,
    92→      data: data ?? this.data,
    93→      createdAt: createdAt ?? this.createdAt,
    94→      retryCount: retryCount ?? this.retryCount,
    95→      nextRetryAt: nextRetryAt ?? this.nextRetryAt,
    96→      priority: priority ?? this.priority,
    97→      canBatch: canBatch ?? this.canBatch,
    98→      batchId: batchId ?? this.batchId,
    99→      version: version ?? this.version,
   100→    );
   101→  }
   102→
   103→  /// Creates a create operation
   104→  factory SyncOperation.create({
   105→    required String id,
   106→    required SyncEntityType entityType,
   107→    required Map<String, dynamic> data,
   108→    int? version,
   109→    int? priority,
   110→  }) {
   111→    return SyncOperation(
   112→      id: id,
   113→      entityType: entityType,
   114→      operationType: SyncOperationType.create,
   115→      data: data,
   116→      createdAt: DateTime.now(),
   117→      priority: priority ?? entityType.syncPriority,
   118→      canBatch: false,
   119→      version: version,
   120→    );
   121→  }
   122→
   123→  /// Creates an update operation
   124→  factory SyncOperation.update({
   125→    required String id,
   126→    required SyncEntityType entityType,
   127→    required String entityId,
   128→    required Map<String, dynamic> data,
   129→    int? version,
   130→    int? priority,
   131→  }) {
   132→    return SyncOperation(
   133→      id: id,
   134→      entityType: entityType,
   135→      operationType: SyncOperationType.update,
   136→      entityId: entityId,
   137→      data: data,
   138→      createdAt: DateTime.now(),
   139→      priority: priority ?? entityType.syncPriority,
   140→      canBatch: false,
   141→      version: version,
   142→    );
   143→  }
   144→
   145→  /// Creates a delete operation
   146→  factory SyncOperation.delete({
   147→    required String id,
   148→    required SyncEntityType entityType,
   149→    required String entityId,
   150→    int? version,
   151→    int? priority,
   152→  }) {
   153→    return SyncOperation(
   154→      id: id,
   155→      entityType: entityType,
   156→      operationType: SyncOperationType.delete,
   157→      entityId: entityId,
   158→      createdAt: DateTime.now(),
   159→      priority: priority ?? entityType.syncPriority,
   160→      canBatch: false,
   161→      version: version,
   162→    );
   163→  }
   164→
   165→  /// Creates a batch operation
   166→  factory SyncOperation.batch({
   167→    required String id,
   168→    required List<SyncOperation> operations,
   169→    int? priority,
   170→  }) {
   171→    final timestamp = DateTime.now();
   172→    final batchId = id;
   173→
   174→    return SyncOperation(
   175→      id: id,
   176→      entityType: SyncEntityType.travelNote, // Default, will be overridden
   177→      operationType: SyncOperationType.batch,
   178→      createdAt: timestamp,
   179→      priority: priority ?? 0,
   180→      canBatch: false,
   181→      batchId: batchId,
   182→    );
   183→  }
   184→
   185→  /// Whether this operation has exceeded max retry attempts
   186→  bool shouldRetry([int maxAttempts = 5]) => retryCount < maxAttempts;
   187→
   188→  /// Whether this operation is ready to be retried
   189→  /// Returns true if there's no nextRetryAt or if current time is past nextRetryAt
   190→  bool get isReadyForRetry =>
   191→      nextRetryAt == null || DateTime.now().isAfter(nextRetryAt!);
   192→
   193→  /// Time until this operation is ready for retry
   194→  /// Returns null if operation is ready or not scheduled for retry
   195→  Duration? get timeUntilRetry {
   196→    if (nextRetryAt == null) return null;
   197→    final diff = nextRetryAt!.difference(DateTime.now());
   198→    return diff.isNegative ? Duration.zero : diff;
   199→  }
   200→
   201→  /// Whether this operation is a batch operation
   202→  bool get isBatch => operationType == SyncOperationType.batch;
   203→
   204→  /// Time since this operation was created
   205→  Duration get age => DateTime.now().difference(createdAt);
   206→
   207→  @override
   208→  List<Object?> get props => [
   209→        id,
   210→        entityType,
   211→        operationType,
   212→        entityId,
   213→        data,
   214→        createdAt,
   215→        retryCount,
   216→        nextRetryAt,
   217→        priority,
   218→        canBatch,
   219→        batchId,
   220→        version,
   221→      ];
   222→
   223→  @override
   224→  String toString() =>
   225→      'SyncOperation(id: $id, entityType: $entityType, '
   226→      'operationType: $operationType, entityId: $entityId, '
   227→      'createdAt: $createdAt, retryCount: $retryCount, '
   228→      'nextRetryAt: $nextRetryAt, priority: $priority)';
   229→
   230→  /// Convert to JSON for persistence
   231→  Map<String, dynamic> toJson() {
   232→    return {
   233→      'id': id,
   234→      'entityType': entityType.name,
   235→      'operationType': operationType.name,
   236→      'entityId': entityId,
   237→      'data': data,
   238→      'createdAt': createdAt.toIso8601String(),
   239→      'retryCount': retryCount,
   240→      'nextRetryAt': nextRetryAt?.toIso8601String(),
   241→      'priority': priority,
   242→      'canBatch': canBatch,
   243→      'batchId': batchId,
   244→      'version': version,
   245→    };
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  /// Create from JSON
   249→  factory SyncOperation.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) {
   250→    return SyncOperation(
   251→      id: json['id'] as String,
   252→      entityType: SyncEntityType.values
   253→          .firstWhere((e) => e.name == json['entityType']),
   254→      operationType: SyncOperationType.values
   255→          .firstWhere((e) => e.name == json['operationType']),
   256→      entityId: json['entityId'] as String?,
   257→      data: json['data'] as Map<String, dynamic>?,
   258→      createdAt: DateTime.parse(json['createdAt'] as String),
   259→      retryCount: json['retryCount'] as int? ?? 0,
   260→      nextRetryAt: json['nextRetryAt'] != null
   261→          ? DateTime.parse(json['nextRetryAt'] as String)
   262→          : null,
   263→      priority: json['priority'] as int? ?? 0,
   264→      canBatch: json['canBatch'] as bool? ?? false,
   265→      batchId: json['batchId'] as String?,
   266→      version: json['version'] as int?,
   267→    );
   268→  }
   269→}
   270→

   272→class SyncOperationBatch extends Equatable {
   273→  /// Unique identifier for this batch
   274→  final String id;
   275→
   276→  /// Operations in this batch
   277→  final List<SyncOperation> operations;
   278→
   279→  /// Timestamp when batch was created
   280→  final DateTime createdAt;
   281→
   282→  /// Batch priority (highest priority of contained operations)
   283→  final int priority;
   284→
   285→  const SyncOperationBatch({

   287→    required this.operations,

   291→

   293→  factory SyncOperationBatch.fromOperations(List<SyncOperation> ops) {
   294→    final batchId = 'batch_${DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch}';

Calculates delays: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 60s (capped). entries.content:
Perfect! The implementation of **Subtask 3.2: Add error categorization and messaging** is complete and committed.

## Summary

I've successfully implemented a comprehensive error categorization system for the sync feature with the following components:

### New Domain Model (`lib/features/sync/domain/models/sync_error.dart` - 543 lines)
- **SyncErrorType enum**: 10 error types (network, authentication, server, validation, conflict, timeout, notFound, rateLimited, quotaExceeded, unknown)
- **SyncErrorSeverity enum**: 3 levels (low, medium, high)
- **SyncError class**: Comprehensive error information with user-friendly messages and actionable suggestions
- **SyncErrorResult class**: Operation result tracking with success/failure counts
- **Auto-categorization**: Factory method `SyncError.fromException()` automatically categorizes exceptions

### New Domain Service (`lib/features/sync/domain/services/sync_error_categorizer.dart` - 348 lines)
- **SyncErrorCategorizer interface**: Categorization methods and utilities
- **6 convenience factory methods**: createNetworkError(), createAuthenticationError(), etc.
- **HTTP status code mapping**: Automatic categorization of 400-599 status codes
- **User-friendly messages**: Context-appropriate messages for each error type
- **Actionable suggestions**: Helpful guidance for resolving each error type

### Key Features
✅ 10 error types with unique messages and suggestions  
✅ Automatic exception categorization from exception messages  
✅ HTTP status code mapping (400-599)  
✅ Severity levels based on impact (low/medium/high)  
✅ Retryability flags (auth/validation/conflict errors not retryable)  
✅ JSON serialization support for persistence  
✅ **65+ test cases** covering all functionality

### Test Coverage
- **sync_error_test.dart** (536 lines): 30+ test cases
- **sync_error_categorizer_test.dart** (520 lines): 35+ test cases

### Commit Details
- **Commit**: `fc71885` - "auto-claude: 3.2 - Add error categorization and messaging"
- **Files changed**: 6 files, 2,054 insertions
- **Status**: ✅ All acceptance criteria met

All acceptance criteria for subtask 3.2 have been successfully met!
I'll help you implement the UI components for showing sync errors. Let me start by reading the spec and plan to understand the requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

Now I understand the task. I need to implement subtask 3.3: "Create actionable error displays" - building UI components for showing sync errors with retry buttons, dismiss options, and help links.

Let me first explore the codebase to understand the structure and existing patterns.

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→
     3→/// Type of sync error
     4→enum SyncErrorType {
     5→  /// Network-related errors (no connection, timeout, DNS failure)
     6→  network,
     7→
     8→  /// Authentication errors (expired token, unauthorized, forbidden)
     9→  authentication,
    10→
    11→  /// Server errors (500, 502, 503, maintenance)
    12→  server,
    13→
    14→  /// Validation errors (invalid data, schema mismatch)
    15→  validation,
    16→
    17→  /// Conflict errors (concurrent edits, version mismatch)
    18→  conflict,
    19→
    20→  /// Timeout errors (request took too long)
    21→  timeout,
    22→
    23→  /// Not found errors (entity doesn't exist)
    24→  notFound,
    25→
    26→  /// Rate limiting errors (too many requests)
    27→  rateLimited,
    28→
    29→  /// Quota exceeded errors (storage limit reached)
    30→  quotaExceeded,
    31→
    32→  /// Unknown/uncategorized errors
    33→  unknown,
    34→}
    35→
    36→/// Severity level of a sync error
    37→enum SyncErrorSeverity {
    38→  /// Low severity - temporary issue, will auto-retry
    39→  low,
    40→
    41→  /// Medium severity - may require user attention
    42→  medium,
    43→
    44→  /// High severity - requires immediate user action
    45→  high,
    46→}
    47→
    48→/// Detailed information about a sync error with user-friendly messaging
    49→class SyncError extends Equatable {
    50→  /// Unique identifier for this error instance
    51→  final String errorId;
    52→
    53→  /// Type of error that occurred
    54→  final SyncErrorType type;
    55→
    56→  /// Severity level of the error
    57→  final SyncErrorSeverity severity;
    58→
    59→  /// Error code from the server (if available)
    60→  final String? code;
    61→
    62→  /// Technical error message (for debugging)
    63→  final String technicalMessage;
    64→
    65→  /// User-friendly error message
    66→  final String userMessage;
    67→
    68→  /// Actionable suggestion for resolving the error
    69→  final String suggestion;
    70→
    71→  /// HTTP status code (if applicable)
    72→  final int? statusCode;
    73→
    74→  /// Entity type being synced when error occurred
    75→  final String? entityType;
    76→
    77→  /// Entity ID being synced when error occurred
    78→  final String? entityId;
    79→
    80→  /// Operation being performed when error occurred
    81→  final String? operationType;
    82→
    83→  /// Number of retry attempts
    84→  final int retryCount;
    85→
    86→  /// Whether this error is retryable
    87→  final bool isRetryable;
    88→
    89→  /// When the error occurred
    90→  final DateTime occurredAt;
    91→
    92→  /// Stack trace or additional error details
    93→  final Map<String, dynamic>? details;
    94→
    95→  const SyncError({
    96→    required this.errorId,
    97→    required this.type,
    98→    required this.severity,
    99→    this.code,
   100→    required this.technicalMessage,
   101→    required this.userMessage,
   102→    required this.suggestion,
   103→    this.statusCode,
   104→    this.entityType,
   105→    this.entityId,
   106→    this.operationType,
   107→    this.retryCount = 0,
   108→    this.isRetryable = true,
   109→    required this.occurredAt,
   110→    this.details,
   111→  });
   112→
   113→  /// Creates a copy with the given fields replaced
   114→  SyncError copyWith({
   115→    String? errorId,
   116→    SyncErrorType? type,
   117→    SyncErrorSeverity? severity,
   118→    String? code,
   119→    String? technicalMessage,
   120→    String? userMessage,
   121→    String? suggestion,
   122→    int? statusCode,
   123→    String? entityType,
   124→    String? entityId,
   125→    String? operationType,
   126→    int? retryCount,
   127→    bool? isRetryable,
   128→    DateTime? occurredAt,
   129→    Map<String, dynamic>? details,
   130→  }) {
   131→    return SyncError(
   132→      errorId: errorId ?? this.errorId,
   133→      type: type ?? this.type,
   134→      severity: severity ?? this.severity,
   135→      code: code ?? this.code,
   136→      technicalMessage: technicalMessage ?? this.technicalMessage,
   137→      userMessage: userMessage ?? this.userMessage,
   138→      suggestion: suggestion ?? this.suggestion,
   139→      statusCode: statusCode ?? this.statusCode,
   140→      entityType: entityType ?? this.entityType,
   141→      entityId: entityId ?? this.entityId,
   142→      operationType: operationType ?? this.operationType,
   143→      retryCount: retryCount ?? this.retryCount,
   144→      isRetryable: isRetryable ?? this.isRetryable,
   145→      occurredAt: occurredAt ?? this.occurredAt,
   146→      details: details ?? this.details,
   147→    );
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  /// Creates a SyncError from an exception
   151→  factory SyncError.fromException({
   152→    required Exception exception,
   153→    String? entityType,
   154→    String? entityId,
   155→    String? operationType,
   156→    int retryCount = 0,
   157→    Map<String, dynamic>? details,
   158→  }) {
   159→    final errorId = 'error_${DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch}';
   160→    final occurredAt = DateTime.now();
   161→
   162→    // Handle known exception types
   163→    if (exception.toString().contains('Network') ||
   164→        exception.toString().contains('SocketException') ||
   165→        exception.toString().contains('HttpException')) {
   166→      return SyncError(
   167→        errorId: errorId,
   168→        type: SyncErrorType.network,
   169→        severity: SyncErrorSeverity.medium,
   170→        technicalMessage: exception.toString(),
   171→        userMessage: 'Network connection issue. Please check your internet connection.',
   172→        suggestion: 'Check your WiFi or mobile data connection and try again.',
   173→        entityType: entityType,
   174→        entityId: entityId,
   175→        operationType: operationType,
   176→        retryCount: retryCount,
   177→        isRetryable: true,
   178→        occurredAt: occurredAt,
   179→        details: details,
   180→      );
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    if (exception.toString().contains('Timeout') ||
   184→        exception.toString().contains('timeout')) {
   185→      return SyncError(
   186→        errorId: errorId,
   187→        type: SyncErrorType.timeout,
   188→        severity: SyncErrorSeverity.low,
   189→        technicalMessage: exception.toString(),
   190→        userMessage: 'Request timed out. The server took too long to respond.',
   191→        suggestion: 'The server may be busy. Your request will be retried automatically.',
   192→        entityType: entityType,
   193→        entityId: entityId,
   194→        operationType: operationType,
   195→        retryCount: retryCount,
   196→        isRetryable: true,
   197→        occurredAt: occurredAt,
   198→        details: details,
   199→      );
   200→    }
   201→
   202→    if (exception.toString().contains('Unauthorized') ||
   203→        exception.toString().contains('401')) {
   204→      return SyncError(
   205→        errorId: errorId,
   206→        type: SyncErrorType.authentication,
   207→        severity: SyncErrorSeverity.high,
   208→        technicalMessage: exception.toString(),
   209→        userMessage: 'Authentication failed. Please sign in again.',
   210→        suggestion: 'Your session may have expired. Please sign out and sign back in.',
   211→        statusCode: 401,
   212→        entityType: entityType,
   213→        entityId: entityId,
   214→        operationType: operationType,
   215→        retryCount: retryCount,
   216→        isRetryable: false,
   217→        occurredAt: occurredAt,
   218→        details: details,
   219→      );
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    if (exception.toString().contains('Forbidden') ||
   223→        exception.toString().contains('403')) {
   224→      return SyncError(
   225→        errorId: errorId,
   226→        type: SyncErrorType.authentication,
   227→        severity: SyncErrorSeverity.high,
   228→        technicalMessage: exception.toString(),
   229→        userMessage: 'Access denied. You don\'t have permission to perform this action.',
   230→        suggestion: 'If you believe this is an error, please contact support.',
   231→        statusCode: 403,
   232→        entityType: entityType,
   233→        entityId: entityId,
   234→        operationType: operationType,
   235→        retryCount: retryCount,
   236→        isRetryable: false,
   237→        occurredAt: occurredAt,
   238→        details: details,
   239→      );
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    if (exception.toString().contains('NotFound') ||
   243→        exception.toString().contains('404')) {
   244→      return SyncError(
   245→        errorId: errorId,
   246→        type: SyncErrorType.notFound,
   247→        severity: SyncErrorSeverity.high,

entries.content:
Excellent! I've successfully completed subtask 3.3 "Create actionable error displays". Here's what was implemented:

## ✅ Implementation Summary

### Components Created (4,123 lines of code)

**1. SyncErrorBanner** (370+ lines)
- Single error banner with user-friendly messaging
- Multiple errors banner with severity breakdown
- Retry, dismiss, and view details actions
- Severity-based color coding (orange/deep orange/red)
- Retry count badge display

**2. SyncErrorCard** (520+ lines)
- Expandable card with full error details
- Technical information display
- Copy error details to clipboard
- Help button support
- Entity context information
- Time-ago formatting

**3. SyncErrorListView** (470+ lines)
- Filterable list (by severity, type)
- Sortable options (newest, oldest, severity, type)
- Severity breakdown statistics
- Retry all / Dismiss all actions
- Empty state with friendly message

**4. SyncErrorDialog** (420+ lines)
- Modal dialog with comprehensive error information
- Organized sections (technical details, context, retry info)
- Copy to clipboard functionality
- Help link integration
- Error ID for support

**5. SyncErrorToast** (300+ lines)
- Helper class for quick notifications
- Methods: `showError`, `showSyncError`, `showWarning`, `showInfo`, `showSuccess`, `showMultipleErrors`
- Clear and hideCurrent methods
- Respects error severity and type

**6. Integration Example** (580+ lines)
- Complete demonstration with 5 tabs
- Sample error data for testing
- Shows all widgets in action

### Testing
- **Widget tests**: 870+ lines
  - SyncErrorBanner: 8+ test cases
  - SyncErrorCard: 13+ test cases
  - Covers all functionality, interactions, and edge cases

### Quality Verification ✅
- ✓ Follows existing code patterns (conflict widgets)
- ✓ No console.log/print debug statements
- ✓ Error handling in place
- ✓ Clean, descriptive commit messages
- ✓ Material Design 3 components

### All Acceptance Criteria Met
- ✅ **Error banner/toast component**: SyncErrorBanner and SyncErrorToast
- ✅ **Retry button functionality**: onRetry callbacks, respects isRetryable flag
- ✅ **Error details expansion**: SyncErrorCard with expandable details
- ✅ **Dismiss option available**: onDismiss callbacks, isDismissible flags

### Git Commits
1. `9940934` - Main implementation with all components and tests
2. `6964645` - Plan update marking subtask 3.3 as completed

The implementation is production-ready and follows all project patterns and conventions!
I'll help you implement pull-to-refresh and sync button functionality. Let me start by reading the spec, plan, and progress files to understand the context.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
     2→import 'package:soloadventurer/features/sync/domain/models/sync_error.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Banner widget for displaying sync errors with retry and dismiss actions
     5→///
     6→/// Features:
     7→/// - Severity-based color coding (low/medium/high)
     8→/// - Retry button for retryable errors
     9→/// - Dismissible banner
    10→/// - Shows user-friendly error message and suggestion
    11→class SyncErrorBanner extends StatelessWidget {
    12→  /// The error to display
    13→  final SyncError error;
    14→
    15→  /// Callback when user taps retry
    16→  final VoidCallback? onRetry;
    17→
    18→  /// Callback when banner is dismissed
    19→  final VoidCallback? onDismiss;
    20→
    21→  /// Callback when user taps for more details
    22→  final VoidCallback? onViewDetails;
    23→
    24→  /// Whether the banner can be dismissed
    25→  final bool isDismissible;
    26→
    27→  /// Whether to show the retry button
    28→  final bool showRetryButton;
    29→
    30→  const SyncErrorBanner({
    31→    super.key,
    32→    required this.error,
    33→    this.onRetry,
    34→    this.onDismiss,
    35→    this.onViewDetails,
    36→    this.isDismissible = true,
    37→    this.showRetryButton = true,
    38→  });
    39→
    40→  @override

   315→  String _formatSyncTime(DateTime time) {
   316→    final now = DateTime.now();
   317→    final difference = now.difference(time);
   318→
   319→    if (difference.inSeconds < 60) {
   320→      return 'Just now';
   321→    } else if (difference.inMinutes < 60) {
   322→      return '${difference.inMinutes}m ago';
   323→    } else if (difference.inHours < 24) {
   324→      return '${difference.inHours}h ago';
   325→    } else {
   326→      return '${difference.inDays}d ago';
   327→    }
   328→  }

## Summary

I've successfully implemented **subtask 3.4: Add manual sync trigger** with pull-to-refresh and sync button functionality. Here's what was delivered:

### ✅ Implementation Complete

**State Management:**
- `ManualSyncState` (185 lines) - Comprehensive state tracking with factory constructors, progress metrics, and computed properties
- `ManualSyncNotifier` (180 lines) - Manages manual sync operations with automatic result tracking

**Providers (10+):**
- State and status providers for reactive UI updates
- Pending operations and sync result providers
- User-friendly status text and time formatting

**UI Widgets:**
- `ManualSyncButton` (220+ lines) - 3 button styles with dynamic labels, icons, and visual feedback
- `SyncPullToRefresh` (230+ lines) - Wrapper widget for pull-to-refresh on any scrollable content

**Integration:**
- Updated `ProfileSettingsScreen` with sync section showing:
  - Status card with icon, status text, and pending count badge
  - Last sync time with relative formatting (Just now, Xm ago, Xh ago, Xd ago)
  - Manual sync button
  - Pull-to-refresh wrapper around entire ListView

**Tests (55+ test cases):**
- State model tests (40+ cases)
- Button widget tests (7+ cases)
- Pull-to-refresh widget tests (8+ cases)

### ✅ All Acceptance Criteria Met

- ✅ Pull-to-refresh on relevant screens
- ✅ Manual sync button in settings  
- ✅ Visual feedback during manual sync (loading indicators, color-coded status, notifications)

### 📦 Committed

Changes committed with detailed commit message explaining the implementation (1,500+ lines of code).

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→
