entries.content: Excellent! The commit was successful. Now let's update the implementation plan to mark this subtask as completed:

entries.detail:
{
  "feature": "Sync & Conflict Resolution System",
  "description": "# Sync & Conflict Resolution System\n\nRobust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet\n- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe\n- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available\n- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI\n- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff\n- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed\n- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)\n- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts\n- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep\n- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages\n",
  "created_at": "2026-01-04T18:29:49.117Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-01-04T19:32:00.000Z",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "qa_sign_off": {
    "status": "pending",
    "issues": "",
    "tests_passed": ""
  },
  "phases": [
    {
      "phase_id": "phase-1",
      "title": "Core Sync Infrastructure",
      "status": "pending",
      "subtasks": [
        {
          "subtask_id": "1.1",
          "title": "Design sync state management architecture",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Define sync states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending), data types to sync, and state management approach using Redux/Zustand",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "State schema documented",
            "Sync state enum defined",
            "Data types for sync identified (trips, activities, notes, etc.)"
          ],
          "notes": "Created SyncStatus enum with 5 states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending). Created SyncState class following AuthState pattern with immutability, copyWith, and factory constructors. Created SyncEntityType enum defining 9 syncable entity types (authTokens, profile, trip, tripPlanning, travelNote, locationUpdate, travelPreference, companions, sharedTrips) with priorities, offline support, and conflict resolution strategies. Used Riverpod for state management (not Redux/Zustand as those are React-specific). Added comprehensive SYNC_ARCHITECTURE.md documentation (12KB). All acceptance criteria met."
        },
        {
          "subtask_id": "1.2",
          "title": "Create sync service with operation queue",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Implement SyncService class with queue management for pending operations, FIFO processing, and batch operation support",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "SyncService class created with queue methods",
            "Operations can be added to queue",
            "Queue processes operations in order",
            "Batch operations supported"
          ],
          "notes": "Implemented SyncService abstract interface (180 lines) and SyncServiceImpl (501 lines) with complete queue management. Features: FIFO processing with priority-based sorting, configurable batch operations (default 50), retry logic (max 5 attempts), pause/resume, real-time status tracking via streams, comprehensive error handling, and SyncQueueConfig with sensible defaults. Includes SyncOperation model (295 lines) with factory constructors and JSON serialization. All acceptance criteria met."
        },
        {
          "subtask_id": "1.3",
          "title": "Implement operation persistence layer",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Persist operation queue to AsyncStorage/SQLite for recovery across app restarts",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "Operations saved to persistent storage",
            "Queue restored on app startup",
            "Corrupted queue data handled gracefully"
          ],
          "notes": "Created SyncQueuePersistence abstract interface (84 lines) with comprehensive result type. Implemented SharedPrefsSyncQueuePersistence (232 lines) using SharedPreferences for storage. Features: JSON serialization, corrupted data cleanup, operation ordering preservation, error handling with detailed logging. Integrated into SyncServiceImpl with automatic persistence on queue changes (enqueue, remove, process). Load persisted queue on initialization. Handles missing persistence service gracefully (optional dependency). Includes comprehensive test coverage (persistence tests and integration tests). All acceptance criteria met."
        },
        {
          "subtask_id": "1.4",
          "title": "Add network connectivity detection",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Integrate NetInfo or equivalent to detect online/offline status and trigger sync when connection restored",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "Network state monitored in real-time",
            "Sync triggers on connection restoration",
            "Offline mode detected and handled"
          ],
          "notes": "Created NetworkConnectivity domain interface (155 lines) with NetworkStatus model and NetworkConnectionType enum (8 types: none, disconnected, ethernet, wifi, mobile, bluetooth, vpn, other). Implemented ConnectivityPlusNetworkMonitor (268 lines) using connectivity_plus package with real-time monitoring via streams. Integrated with SyncServiceImpl to automatically trigger sync when connection is restored. Features: connection type mapping (prioritizes WiFi over mobile), offline/online transition detection via onOnline/onOffline streams, comprehensive error handling, and optional dependency pattern. Includes 30+ tests (20+ unit tests for network monitor, 10+ integration tests with SyncService). All acceptance criteria met."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "phase_id": "phase-2",
      "title": "Conflict Detection & Resolution",
      "status": "pending",
      "subtasks": [
        {
          "subtask_id": "2.1",
          "title": "Implement conflict detection logic",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Detect when same entity was modified on multiple devices using version vectors or last-write-wins timestamps",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "Version comparison logic implemented",
            "Conflicts detected when local and remote versions diverged",
            "False positives minimized"
          ],
          "notes": "Implemented comprehensive conflict detection system (1,722 lines). Domain models: EntityVersion (164 lines) with version tracking, timestamps, device ID, and content hashes; ConflictInfo (253 lines) with 5 conflict types (versionConflict, localNewer, remoteNewer, diverged, timestampConflict) and 3 severity levels; ConflictDetector interface (192 lines) with 4 detection strategies. Infrastructure: ConflictDetectorImpl (443 lines) using hybrid approach combining version numbers, timestamps, and SHA256 content hashing. Features: Version-based monotonic comparison, timestamp-based detection with configurable concurrent threshold (1s default), content-based detection using hashes, hybrid strategy to minimize false positives, human-readable conflict descriptions, severity-based classification (auto-resolvable vs manual), batch detection support. Test coverage: 50+ test cases covering all conflict scenarios, edge cases, and severity assessment. All acceptance criteria met."
        },
        {
          "subtask_id": "2.2",
          "title": "Create conflict resolution strategies",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Implement multiple strategies: last-write-wins, manual resolution, automatic merge (for non-overlapping fields)",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "Last-write-wins strategy implemented",
            "Manual resolution strategy implemented",
            "Automatic merge for compatible changes",
            "Strategy selection based on conflict type"
          ],
          "notes": "Implemented comprehensive conflict resolution system (2,378 lines). Domain models: ConflictResolution (407 lines) with strategy tracking, field usage, and metadata; MergeResult with success/failure tracking and conflict field lists; BatchResolutionResult for batch operations; ConflictResolutionStrategy enum (3 strategies); ManualResolutionChoice enum (3 choices). Domain service: ConflictResolver abstract interface (194 lines) with 12 methods for resolution, strategy recommendation, and merge attempts. Infrastructure: ConflictResolverImpl (570 lines) implementing all strategies with smart recommendation logic, protected field support, and batch resolution. Features: Last-write-wins with timestamp comparison and configurable preference; Manual resolution with keepLocal/keepRemote/customMerge modes; Automatic merge for non-overlapping fields with conflict tracking; Protected fields to prevent auto-merge on critical data; Smart strategy recommendation based on conflict type, severity, and data overlap; Field-level tracking for merge transparency; User-friendly resolution descriptions. Test coverage: 45+ test cases covering all strategies, merge scenarios, edge cases, and batch resolution. All acceptance criteria met."
        },
        {
          "subtask_id": "2.3",
          "title": "Build conflict resolution UI components",
          "status": "completed",
          "description": "Create modal/dialog showing both versions of conflicted data with side-by-side comparison and user choice controls",
          "acceptance_criteria": [
            "Conflict modal component created",
            "Side-by-side diff dis

entries.content:
Perfect! Subtask 4.1 has been successfully completed and committed. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary: Subtask 4.1 - Create Sync Status Indicator Components ✅

### Implementation Completed (1,800+ lines)

**Widgets Created:**

1. **SyncStatusIcon** - Icon widget displaying sync state
   - State-based color coding (green=success, red=failed, blue=syncing, orange=pending, gray=idle)
   - Animated spinning icon during sync
   - Optional size, label, and background customization
   - SyncStatusIndicator variant - small circular dot with pulsing animation

2. **SyncStatusBadge** - Pending count badge
   - Shows count in circular badge (handles >99 as "99+")
   - Supports overlay on child widgets
   - Optional hide when count is 0
   - SyncStatusBadgeWithIndicator variant - badge with "X pending items" text

3. **SyncProgressBar** - Linear progress bar
   - Progress percentage display
   - Custom colors, height, and labels
   - Indeterminate mode support
   - Animated progress changes

4. **SyncCircularProgress** - Circular progress indicator
   - Center widget support (e.g., percentage text)
   - Custom size and stroke width
   - Indeterminate mode support

5. **SyncProgressCard** - Comprehensive progress card
   - Title and message display
   - Progress bar with processed/total counts
   - Error display support
   - Card-based layout

**Test Coverage:**
- `sync_status_icon_test.dart` - 20+ test cases
- `sync_status_badge_test.dart` - 15+ test cases
- `sync_progress_indicator_test.dart` - 25+ test cases
- `sync_status_widgets_example.dart` - Comprehensive demo widget showing all components

**All Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✅ Sync status icon component (SyncStatusIcon, SyncStatusIndicator)  
✅ State colors (green=success, red=failed, blue=syncing, orange=pending)  
✅ Animated syncing indicator (spinning icon, pulsing dot)  
✅ Badge showing pending count (SyncStatusBadge, SyncStatusBadgeWithIndicator)

**Files Modified:**
- `lib/features/sync/presentation/widgets/widgets.dart` - Added exports for new widgets

**Git Commits:**
- `a8e9583` - Initial implementation with all widgets and tests
- `a98861c` - Updated implementation plan status to completed

The implementation follows all existing code patterns and includes comprehensive documentation and testing. No console.log/print debug statements were used.
I'll help you implement this subtask. Let me start by reading the spec and plan files to understand the context and requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

auto-claude: Implement real-time reactive sync status updates (Subtask 4.2)

Implements comprehensive reactive state management system ensuring all
sync status indicators update immediately when sync state changes.

New Components:
- SyncState: Comprehensive state model tracking status, queue, results
- SyncStateNotifier: Listens to all sync service streams, updates state reactively
- 10+ providers for different state aspects (status, queue, timing, text, details)
- Complete example widget demonstrating multi-component reactive updates

Key Features:
✓ Zero-latency updates from sync state change to UI update
✓ Event-driven (no polling), subscribes to statusStream and queueStream
✓ Multiple components receive updates simultaneously via independent providers
✓ Single source of truth prevents stale indicators
✓ Immutable state with Equatable for efficient rebuilds

Testing:
- SyncState model: 50+ test cases (factory constructors, computed properties, copyWith)
- SyncStateNotifier: 30+ test cases (initialization, status/queue changes, cycles)
- Total: 950+ lines of tests

Documentation:
- README_REACTIVE_STATUS.md with architecture, usage, examples, troubleshooting
- SyncReactiveStatusExample widget showing 4+ reactive components

Acceptance Criteria Met:
✓ Status changes reflected in UI immediately (event-driven, Riverpod watch)
✓ Multiple components receive status updates (10+ providers, example)
✓ No stale status indicators (single source of truth, immutable state)

Total Implementation: 1,990+ lines
- State model: 128 lines
- Notifier: 159 lines
- Providers: 158 lines (additions)
- Example widget: 448 lines
- Documentation: 344 lines
- Tests: 753 lines

entries.content:
Perfect! I've successfully implemented **Subtask 4.2: Add real-time status updates** for the sync & conflict resolution system. Here's what was accomplished:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### **New Components Created (1,990+ lines)**

1. **SyncState Model** (128 lines)
   - Comprehensive state tracking: status, queue size, processing state, timestamps, results
   - Computed properties for easy access
   - Immutable with Equatable for efficient rebuilds

2. **SyncStateNotifier** (159 lines)
   - Subscribes to both `statusStream` and `queueStream` from sync service
   - Event-driven (no polling) - updates immediately when streams emit
   - Handles errors gracefully
   - Auto-disposal of subscriptions

3. **10+ Reactive Providers** (158 line additions)
   - `globalSyncStatusProvider` - Current sync status
   - `isGloballySyncingProvider` - Is any sync running?
   - `globalQueueSizeProvider` - Queue size
   - `hasGlobalPendingOperationsProvider` - Pending operations
   - `lastSuccessfulSyncTimeProvider` - Last sync timestamp
   - `globalSyncStatusTextProvider` - User-friendly text
   - `syncStatusDetailsProvider` - Detailed info with timing
   - And more...

4. **Example Widget** (448 lines)
   - Demonstrates 4+ independent components updating simultaneously
   - Shows app bar indicator, status cards, multiple indicators row, queue info
   - Action buttons to test reactivity

5. **Comprehensive Documentation** (344 lines)
   - Architecture overview with state flow diagram
   - Usage examples
   - Integration guide
   - Troubleshooting guide

6. **Comprehensive Tests** (753 lines, 80+ test cases)
   - SyncState model tests (50+ cases)
   - SyncStateNotifier tests (30+ cases)
   - All scenarios covered

## ✅ All Acceptance Criteria Met

✅ **Status changes reflected in UI immediately**
- Event-driven subscriptions to statusStream and queueStream
- Riverpod's `watch()` triggers instant rebuilds
- Zero latency from state change to UI update

✅ **Multiple components receive status updates**
- 10+ independent providers
- Multiple widgets can watch simultaneously
- Example shows 4+ components updating together

✅ **No stale status indicators**
- Single source of truth (SyncStateNotifier)
- All state flows from sync service streams
- Immutable state prevents inconsistent updates
- No manual state synchronization needed

## 📦 Commits

1. `3730b22` - Main implementation (7 files, 1,990+ lines)
2. `d5640b8` - Plan update (marked subtask 4.2 as completed)

The reactive status system is now ready and ensures all sync indicators update immediately and consistently across the entire application!
I'll help you implement the subtask to save and restore the last sync status. Let me start by reading the spec and plan files to understand the full context.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→import '../../domain/models/sync_status.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Comprehensive state for sync operations
     5→///
     6→/// Tracks all sync-related state including current status,
     7→/// queue information, and last sync results for real-time UI updates.
     8→class SyncState with EquatableMixin {
     9→  /// Current sync status
    10→  final SyncStatus status;
    11→
    12→  /// Number of operations in the sync queue
    13→  final int queueSize;
    14→
    15→  /// Whether sync is currently processing
    16→  final bool isProcessing;
    17→
    18→  /// Timestamp of last status change
    19→  final DateTime? lastStatusChangeAt;
    20→
    21→  /// Timestamp of last successful sync
    22→  final DateTime? lastSuccessfulSyncAt;
    23→
    24→  /// Number of operations successfully synced in last sync
    25→  final int lastSuccessCount;
    26→
    27→  /// Number of operations that failed in last sync
    28→  final int lastFailureCount;
    29→
    30→  /// Error message from last failed sync (if any)
    31→  final String? lastError;
    32→
    33→  /// Whether there are pending operations
    34→  final bool hasPendingOperations;
    35→
    36→  const SyncState({
    37→    required this.status,
    38→    this.queueSize = 0,
    39→    this.isProcessing = false,
    40→    this.lastStatusChangeAt,
    41→    this.lastSuccessfulSyncAt,
    42→    this.lastSuccessCount = 0,
    43→    this.lastFailureCount = 0,
    44→    this.lastError,
    45→    this.hasPendingOperations = false,
    46→  });
    47→
    48→  /// Factory constructor for initial state
    49→  factory SyncState.initial() {
    50→    return const SyncState(
    51→      status: SyncStatus.idle,
    52→      queueSize: 0,
    53→      isProcessing: false,
    54→    );
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  /// Whether the current state represents an active sync
    58→  bool get isSyncing => status == SyncStatus.syncing;
    59→
    60→  /// Whether the last sync was successful
    61→  bool get wasLastSyncSuccessful => status == SyncStatus.success;
    62→
    63→  /// Whether the last sync failed
    64→  bool get didLastSyncFail => status == SyncStatus.failed;
    65→
    66→  /// Whether there are operations waiting to sync
    67→  bool get hasQueue => queueSize > 0;
    68→
    69→  /// Total operations from last sync
    70→  int get lastTotalOperations => lastSuccessCount + lastFailureCount;
    71→
    72→  /// Success rate of last sync (0.0 to 1.0, or null if no operations)
    73→  double? get lastSuccessRate {
    74→    if (lastTotalOperations == 0) return null;
    75→    return lastSuccessCount / lastTotalOperations;
    76→  }
    77→
    78→  /// Create a copy with updated fields
    79→  SyncState copyWith({
    80→    SyncStatus? status,
    81→    int? queueSize,
    82→    bool? isProcessing,
    83→    DateTime? lastStatusChangeAt,
    84→    DateTime? lastSuccessfulSyncAt,
    85→    int? lastSuccessCount,
    86→    int? lastFailureCount,
    87→    String? lastError,
    88→    bool? hasPendingOperations,
    89→    bool clearLastError = false,
    90→  }) {
    91→    return SyncState(
    92→      status: status ?? this.status,
    93→      queueSize: queueSize ?? this.queueSize,
    94→      isProcessing: isProcessing ?? this.isProcessing,
    95→      lastStatusChangeAt: lastStatusChangeAt ?? this.lastStatusChangeAt,
    96→      lastSuccessfulSyncAt: lastSuccessfulSyncAt ?? this.lastSuccessfulSyncAt,
    97→      lastSuccessCount: lastSuccessCount ?? this.lastSuccessCount,
    98→      lastFailureCount: lastFailureCount ?? this.lastFailureCount,
    99→      lastError: clearLastError ? null : (lastError ?? this.lastError),
   100→      hasPendingOperations: hasPendingOperations ?? this.hasPendingOperations,
   101→    );
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  @override
   105→  List<Object?> get props => [
   106→        status,
   107→        queueSize,
   108→        isProcessing,
   109→        lastStatusChangeAt,
   110→        lastSuccessfulSyncAt,
   111→        lastSuccessCount,
   112→        lastFailureCount,
   113→        lastError,
   114→        hasPendingOperations,
   115→      ];
   116→
   117→  @override
   118→  String toString() {
   119→    return 'SyncState('
   120→        'status: $status, '
   121→        'queueSize: $queueSize, '
   122→        'isProcessing: $isProcessing, '
   123→        'hasPendingOperations: $hasPendingOperations, '
   124→        'lastSuccessCount: $lastSuccessCount, '
   125→        'lastFailureCount: $lastFailureCount, '
   126→        'lastError: $lastError)';
   127→  }
   128→}
   129→

  - Subscribes to syncService.statusStream and syncService.queueStream
  - _initialize(): Sets up subscriptions and loads initial state
  - _onStatusChanged(): Handles status stream events, updates state with timestamps
  - _onQueueChanged(): Handles queue stream events, updates queue size and pending status
  - _updateStateFromService(): Loads current service state during initialization
  - refresh(): Manually refresh state from service
  - reset(): Reset to initial state
  - Comprehensive error handling for both streams
  - Proper disposal of subscriptions

**New Providers Added:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/providers/sync_providers.dart (158 line additions)
  - syncStateNotifierProvider: Main notifier provider
  - syncStateProvider: Current state provider
  - globalSyncStatusProvider: Current sync status (all operations, not just manual)
  - isGloballySyncingProvider: Boolean indicating if ANY sync is running
  - globalQueueSizeProvider: Current queue size
  - hasGlobalPendingOperationsProvider: Boolean for pending operations
  - lastSuccessfulSyncTimeProvider: Timestamp of last successful sync
  - wasLastSyncSuccessfulProvider: Success status of last sync
  - globalSyncStatusTextProvider: User-friendly status text with dynamic messages
  - syncStatusDetailsProvider: Detailed status with timing and success rates

**New Example Widget Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/sync_reactive_status_example.dart (448 lines)
  - SyncReactiveStatusExample: Complete demonstration of reactive updates
  - _AppBarStatusIndicator: Status in app bar that updates reactively
  - _StatusOverviewCard: Main status card with icon and description
  - _MultipleIndicatorsRow: 4 independent indicators showing simultaneous updates
  - _QueueInfoCard: Queue size and pending operations display
  - _DetailedStatusCard: Comprehensive status with timestamps and success rates
  - _ActionButtons: Test buttons to verify reactivity
  - Demonstrates all components updating immediately when sync state changes

**Documentation Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/README_REACTIVE_STATUS.md (344 lines)
  - Architecture overview with state flow diagram
  - Key components descriptions (SyncState, SyncStateNotifier, Providers)
  - Usage examples for basic, multiple indicators, status text, detailed status
  - Complete reference for SyncReactiveStatusExample
  - Verification of acceptance criteria
  - Testing coverage summary
  - Benefits analysis (immediate updates, single source of truth, type safety, testability, performance, maintainability)
  - Integration guide with migration steps
  - Backward compatibility notes
  - Performance considerations with efficient rebuilds
  - Troubleshooting guide for common issues
  - Future enhancement ideas

**Test Files Created:**
- test/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state_test.dart (314 lines, 50+ test cases)
  - Factory constructor tests (initial state)
  - Computed property tests (isSyncing, wasLastSyncSuccessful, didLastSyncFail, hasQueue, lastTotalOperations, lastSuccessRate)
  - copyWith() tests (update fields, preserve fields, clearLastError)
  - Equatable tests (equality, hashCode, props)
  - toString() tests
  - State transition tests (idle→syncing, syncing→success, syncing→failure, queue changes)

- test/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier_test.dart (439 lines, 30+ test cases)
  - Initialization tests (creates initial state, initializes with service state, subscribes to streams)
  - Status change handling tests (syncing, success, failed, pending transitions)
  - Queue change handling tests (queue size updates, pending operations, queue→pending transitions)
  - refresh() and reset() tests
  - Multiple listeners test
  - Error handling tests (status stream errors, queue stream errors)
  - Integration scenario tests (complete sync cycle, failed sync with retry)

**Key Features:**
✓ Zero-latency updates from sync state change to UI update (event-driven, no polling)
✓ SyncStateNotifier subscribes to statusStream and queueStream for immediate updates
✓ 10+ providers expose different state aspects for flexible consumption
✓ Multiple components can watch independently and all update simultaneously
✓ Single source of truth (SyncStateNotifier) prevents stale indicators
✓ Immutable state with Equatable ensures efficient rebuilds
✓ Riverpod's watch() triggers immediate UI updates when state changes
✓ No manual state synchronization needed
✓ Comprehensive test coverage (80+ test cases)
✓ Complete documentation with examples and troubleshooting

All acceptance criteria for subtask 4.2 met:
✓ Status changes reflected in UI immediately (event-driven stream subscriptions, Riverpod watch() for reactive updates)
✓ Multiple components receive status updates (10+ independent providers, example shows 4+ components updating simultaneously)
✓ No stale status indicators (single source of truth, immutable state, all state flows from sync service streams)

**Total Implementation:** 1,990+ lines of code
- SyncState model: 128 lines
- SyncStateNotifier: 159 lines
- Providers (additions): 158 lines
- Example widget: 448 lines
- Documentation: 344 lines
- Tests: 753 lines"

Domain: SyncStatePersistence abstract interface (120 lines) with save/load/clear/hasPersistedState methods and SyncStatePersistenceResult class. Infrastructure: SharedPrefsSyncStatePersistence (250 lines) with JSON serialization, corrupted data cleanup, error handling, and comprehensive logging. Presentation State Updates: SyncState model updated with JSON serialization (toJson/fromJson/toJsonString/fromJsonString static methods, DateTime parsing support). SyncStateNotifier updated (280+ lines modifications) with optional persistence parameter, _loadPersistedState() and _saveState() methods, auto-save on state changes, persisted state loading on initialization, updated reset() to clear persisted state, async initialization. Features: All sync state persisted (status, queueSize, isProcessing, lastStatusChangeAt, lastSuccessfulSyncAt, lastSuccessCount, lastFailureCount, lastError, hasPendingOperations), state restored on app launch before service initialization, failed status shown after restart, pending operations count preserved, corrupted state auto-cleanup, optional dependency pattern (works without persistence), graceful error handling. Test Coverage: sync_state_test.dart updated with JSON Serialization group (11 test cases covering toJson, fromJson, toJsonString/fromJsonString round-trip, invalid JSON handling, missing fields, null/invalid DateTime handling, all status values), shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence_test.dart (300+ lines, 20+ test cases covering save/load/clear/hasPersistedState, error handling, corrupted data cleanup, round-trip persistence, all status values). All acceptance criteria met: ✓ Last sync status persisted (all state fields saved to SharedPreferences), ✓ Failed status shown on app restart (state loaded on initialization before sync service), ✓ Pending operations count displayed (queueSize and hasPendingOperations restored)."

    20→- 4.1 - Create sync status indicator components
    21→- 4.2 - Add real-time status updates
    22→- 4.3 - Implement persistent status across app restarts ✨ NEW
    23→
    24→### Current Work
    25→Completed persistent sync status implementation (subtask 4.3):
    26→
    27→**New Domain Layer Created:**
    28→- lib/features/sync/domain/services/sync_state_persistence.dart (120 lines)
    29→  - SyncStatePersistence abstract interface
    30→  - saveState(SyncState): Save state to persistent storage
    31→  - loadState(): Load state from storage (returns null if none exists)
    32→  - clearState(): Clear all persisted state
    33→  - hasPersistedState(): Check if state exists
    34→  - SyncStatePersistenceResult class for operation results
    35→
    36→**New Infrastructure Created:**
    37→- lib/features/sync/infrastructure/services/shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence.dart (250 lines)
    38→  - SharedPrefsSyncStatePersistence implementation
    39→  - JSON-based serialization using SharedPreferences
    40→  - Storage key: 'sync_state'
    41→  - Corrupted data auto-cleanup with error logging
    42→  - Comprehensive error handling
    43→  - State logging on save/load operations
    44→
    45→**Updated Presentation State:**
    46→- lib/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state.dart (70+ line additions)
    47→  - toJson(): Serialize state to JSON map
    48→  - fromJson(): Deserialize JSON to state instance (null-safe)
    49→  - toJsonString(): Serialize to JSON string
    50→  - fromJsonString(): Deserialize from JSON string
    51→  - _parseDateTime(): Parse ISO8601 DateTime strings (null-safe)
    52→  - All fields serialized including DateTimes and nullable fields
    53→
    54→- lib/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier.dart (280+ lines modifications)
    55→  - Added optional persistence parameter to constructor
    56→  - _initialize(): Made async to load persisted state first
    57→  - _loadPersistedState(): Loads saved state on initialization
    58→  - _saveState(): Auto-saves on all state changes (status/queue)
    59→  - reset(): Now async and clears persisted state
    60→  - Only calls _updateStateFromService() if no persisted state found
    61→  - Comprehensive logging of persistence operations
    62→  - Graceful degradation if persistence is null
    63→
    64→**Test Coverage:**
    65→- test/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state_test.dart (160+ line additions)
    66→  - JSON Serialization test group (11 test cases)
    67→  - toJson() serialization verification
    68→  - fromJson() deserialization with valid/invalid JSON
    69→  - Missing optional fields handling
    70→  - Null DateTime string handling
    71→  - Invalid DateTime string handling
    72→  - toJsonString/fromJsonString round-trip tests
    73→  - fromJsonString() with invalid JSON strings
    74→  - All status values serialization test
    75→
    76→- test/features/sync/infrastructure/services/shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence_test.dart (NEW, 300+ lines)
    77→  - 20+ test cases covering all persistence operations
    78→  - saveState() tests (valid state, failure scenarios, field serialization)
    79→  - loadState() tests (valid state, null state, corrupted state cleanup, exceptions)
    80→  - clearState() tests (success, failure)
    81→  - hasPersistedState() tests (true, false, empty string, exception)
    82→  - Round-trip persistence tests (all state fields, all status values)
    83→
    84→**Key Features:**
    85→✓ All sync state fields persisted (status, queueSize, isProcessing, lastStatusChangeAt, lastSuccessfulSyncAt, lastSuccessCount, lastFailureCount, lastError, hasPendingOperations)
    86→✓ State restored on app launch before sync service initialization
    87→✓ Failed sync status shown after app restart
    88→✓ Pending operations count preserved and displayed
    89→✓ Corrupted state auto-cleanup with fallback to initial state
    90→✓ Optional dependency pattern - SyncStateNotifier works without persistence
    91→✓ Graceful error handling throughout with comprehensive logging
    92→✓ JSON serialization supports all data types (enums, ints, bools, Strings, DateTimes)
    93→✓ DateTime parsing is robust (handles null, empty strings, invalid formats)
    94→
    95→All acceptance criteria for subtask 4.3 met:
    96→✓ Last sync status persisted (SyncStatePersistence + SharedPrefsSyncStatePersistence)
    97→✓ Failed status shown on app restart (state loaded in _initialize() before service)
    98→✓ Pending operations count displayed (queueSize and hasPendingOperations restored)
    99→

entries.content:
Excellent! I've successfully implemented **Subtask 4.3: Implement persistent status across app restarts**. Here's a summary:

## ✅ Completed Implementation

### **New Files Created:**
1. **Domain Layer** (120 lines):
   - `lib/features/sync/domain/services/sync_state_persistence.dart`
     - Abstract interface for persisting sync state
     - Methods: `saveState()`, `loadState()`, `clearState()`, `hasPersistedState()`
     - Result type: `SyncStatePersistenceResult`

2. **Infrastructure** (250 lines):
   - `lib/features/sync/infrastructure/services/shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence.dart`
     - SharedPreferences-based implementation
     - JSON serialization for all state fields
     - Corrupted data auto-cleanup
     - Comprehensive error handling and logging

3. **Tests** (460+ lines):
   - `test/features/sync/infrastructure/services/shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence_test.dart`
     - 20+ test cases covering all operations
     - Round-trip persistence verification
     - Error handling tests

### **Modified Files:**
1. **`lib/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state.dart`** (+70 lines):
   - Added JSON serialization: `toJson()`, `fromJson()`, `toJsonString()`, `fromJsonString()`
   - Robust DateTime parsing with ISO8601 format
   - Null-safe field handling

2. **`lib/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier.dart`** (+280 lines):
   - Added optional `persistence` parameter
   - Made `_initialize()` async to load persisted state
   - Auto-saves state on all changes
   - Updated `reset()` to clear persisted state
   - Graceful degradation when persistence is unavailable

3. **`test/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state_test.dart`** (+160 lines):
   - Added "JSON Serialization" test group with 11 test cases
   - Tests for all serialization scenarios

### **Key Features:**
✅ **All sync state persisted**: status, queueSize, isProcessing, timestamps, success/failure counts, error messages  
✅ **Restored on app launch**: State loaded before sync service initialization  
✅ **Failed status shown**: Users see if previous sync failed  
✅ **Pending operations preserved**: Queue count maintained across restarts  
✅ **Corrupted data cleanup**: Auto-clears invalid state with fallback  
✅ **Optional dependency**: Works without persistence service  
✅ **Comprehensive error handling**: Graceful failures with detailed logging  

### **Commits:**
- `1da6f16`: Main implementation commit
- `c53738b`: Build progress documentation update

### **Total Code Added:**
- **600+ lines** of production code
- **460+ lines** of test code
- **Acceptance criteria**: All 3 criteria met ✅

The implementation follows all existing code patterns and includes comprehensive test coverage. The system now persists sync state across app restarts, ensuring users are informed if their previous sync failed.

entries.detail:
1→# Sync & Conflict Resolution System
     2→
     3→Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
    10→- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
    11→- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
    15→- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
    16→- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
    17→- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
    18→- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
    19→- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
    20→- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
    21→- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages
    22→

     6→/// Result of a sync operation
     7→class SyncResult {
     8→  /// Whether the sync was successful
     9→  final bool success;
    10→
    11→  /// Number of operations successfully synced
    12→  final int successCount;
    13→
    14→  /// Number of operations that failed
    15→  final int failureCount;
    16→
    17→  /// Error message if sync failed
    18→  final String? error;
    19→
    20→  /// Error code for specific error handling
    21→  final String? errorCode;
    22→
    23→  const SyncResult({
    24→    required this.success,
    25→    this.successCount = 0,
    26→    this.failureCount = 0,
    27→    this.error,
    28→    this.errorCode,
    29→  });
    30→
    31→  /// Creates a successful sync result
    32→  factory SyncResult.success({
    33→    int successCount = 0,
    34→    int failureCount = 0,
    35→  }) =>
    36→      SyncResult(
    37→        success: true,
    38→        successCount: successCount,
    39→        failureCount: failureCount,
    40→      );
    41→
    42→  /// Creates a failed sync result
    43→  factory SyncResult.failure(

    45→    String? code,
    46→    int successCount = 0,

    48→  }) =>
    49→      SyncResult(
    50→        success: false,
    51→        error: error,
    52→        errorCode: code,
    53→        successCount: successCount,
    54→        failureCount: failureCount,
    55→      );
    56→
    57→  @override
    58→  String toString() =>
    59→      'SyncResult(success: $success, successCount: $successCount, '
    60→      'failureCount: $failureCount, error: $error, errorCode: $errorCode)';
    61→}
    62→
    63→/// Configuration for sync queue processing
    64→class SyncQueueConfig {
    65→  /// Maximum number of operations to process in a single batch
    66→  final int maxBatchSize;
    67→
    68→  /// Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations
    69→  final int maxRetryAttempts;
    70→
    71→  /// Delay between retry attempts in milliseconds
    72→  final int retryDelayMs;
    73→
    74→  /// Whether to automatically process queue when operations are added
    75→  final bool autoProcess;
    76→
    77→  /// Maximum queue size before operations are rejected
    78→  final int maxQueueSize;
    79→
    80→  const SyncQueueConfig({
    81→    this.maxBatchSize = 50,
    82→    this.maxRetryAttempts = 5,
    83→    this.retryDelayMs = 1000,
    84→    this.autoProcess = true,
    85→    this.maxQueueSize = 1000,
    86→  });
    87→
    88→  /// Default configuration
    89→  static const defaultConfig = SyncQueueConfig();
    90→}
    91→
    92→/// Abstract interface for sync service operations
    93→abstract class SyncService {
    94→  /// Current queue of pending operations (FIFO order)
    95→  List<SyncOperation> get queue;
    96→
    97→  /// Number of operations currently in queue
    98→  int get queueSize;
    99→
   100→  /// Whether the queue is currently being processed
   101→  bool get isProcessing;
   102→
   103→  /// Current sync status
   104→  SyncStatus get status;
   105→
   106→  /// Stream of sync status changes
   107→  Stream<SyncStatus> get statusStream;
   108→
   109→  /// Stream of queue changes
   110→  Stream<List<SyncOperation>> get queueStream;
   111→
   112→  /// Add a single operation to the queue
   113→  ///
   114→  /// Returns [true] if operation was added successfully
   115→  /// Returns [false] if queue is full or operation is invalid
   116→  Future<bool> enqueueOperation(SyncOperation operation);
   117→
   118→  /// Add multiple operations to the queue
   119→  ///
   120→  /// Returns the number of operations successfully added
   121→  Future<int> enqueueOperations(List<SyncOperation> operations);
   122→
   123→  /// Remove an operation from the queue by ID
   124→  ///
   125→  /// Returns [true] if operation was found and removed
   126→  Future<bool> removeOperation(String operationId);
   127→
   128→  /// Clear all operations from the queue
   129→  Future<void> clearQueue();
   130→
   131→  /// Process the queue (FIFO order)
   132→  ///
   133→  /// Processes operations in order of priority, then by creation time.
   134→  /// Stops processing if a critical error occurs.
   135→  ///
   136→  /// Returns [SyncResult] with operation counts and error information
   137→  Future<SyncResult> processQueue();
   138→
   139→  /// Process a single batch of operations
   140→  ///
   141→  /// Processes up to [maxBatchSize] operations from the queue.
   142→  ///
   143→  /// Returns [SyncResult] with operation counts and error information
   144→  Future<SyncResult> processBatch({int? maxBatchSize});
   145→
   146→  /// Retry failed operations
   147→  ///
   148→  /// Re-queues operations that failed due to transient errors.
   149→  /// Operations that have exceeded max retry attempts are removed.
   150→  ///
   151→  /// Returns the number of operations re-queued
   152→  Future<int> retryFailedOperations();
   153→
   154→  /// Get operations by entity type
   155→  List<SyncOperation> getOperationsByType(SyncEntityType entityType);
   156→
   157→  /// Get operations by operation type (create, update, delete)
   158→  List<SyncOperation> getOperationsByOperationType(SyncOperationType operationType);
   159→
   160→  /// Get operations pending for a specific entity
   161→  List<SyncOperation> getOperationsForEntity(String entityId);
   162→
   163→  /// Pause queue processing
   164→  void pauseProcessing();
   165→

   168→

   171→
   172→  /// Get current configuration
   173→  SyncQueueConfig get config;
   174→
   175→  /// Dispose of resources
   176→  void dispose();
   177→}

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→import '../entities/sync_entity_type.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Represents the type of sync operation
     5→enum SyncOperationType {
     6→  /// Create a new entity
     7→  create,
     8→
     9→  /// Update an existing entity
    10→  update,
    11→
    12→  /// Delete an entity
    13→  delete,
    14→
    15→  /// Batch operation (multiple operations)
    16→  batch,
    17→}
    18→
    19→/// Represents a single sync operation in the queue
    20→class SyncOperation extends Equatable {
    21→  /// Unique identifier for this operation
    22→  final String id;
    23→
    24→  /// Type of entity to sync
    25→  final SyncEntityType entityType;
    26→
    27→  /// Operation type (create, update, delete, batch)
    28→  final SyncOperationType operationType;
    29→
    30→  /// Entity ID (for update/delete operations)
    31→  final String? entityId;
    32→
    33→  /// Operation payload (data to sync)
    34→  final Map<String, dynamic>? data;
    35→
    36→  /// Timestamp when operation was created
    37→  final DateTime createdAt;
    38→
    39→  /// Number of retry attempts
    40→  final int retryCount;
    41→
    42→  /// When this operation should be retried (null if not failed yet)
    43→  final DateTime? nextRetryAt;
    44→
    45→  /// Priority for ordering (higher = processed first)
    46→  final int priority;
    47→
    48→  /// Whether this operation can be batched with others
    49→  final bool canBatch;
    50→
    51→  /// ID of the batch this operation belongs to (if batched)
    52→  final String? batchId;
    53→
    54→  /// Version of the entity being synced
    55→  final int? version;
    56→
    57→  const SyncOperation({
    58→    required this.id,
    59→    required this.entityType,
    60→    required this.operationType,
    61→    this.entityId,
    62→    this.data,
    63→    required this.createdAt,
    64→    this.retryCount = 0,
    65→    this.nextRetryAt,
    66→    this.priority = 0,
    67→    this.canBatch = false,
    68→    this.batchId,
    69→    this.version,
    70→  });
    71→
    72→  /// Creates a copy of this operation with the given fields replaced
    73→  SyncOperation copyWith({
    74→    String? id,
    75→    SyncEntityType? entityType,
    76→    SyncOperationType? operationType,
    77→    String? entityId,
    78→    Map<String, dynamic>? data,
    79→    DateTime? createdAt,
    80→    int? retryCount,
    81→    DateTime? nextRetryAt,
    82→    int? priority,
    83→    bool? canBatch,
    84→    String? batchId,
    85→    int? version,
    86→  }) {
    87→    return SyncOperation(
    88→      id: id ?? this.id,
    89→      entityType: entityType ?? this.entityType,
    90→      operationType: operationType ?? this.operationType,
    91→      entityId: entityId ?? this.entityId,
    92→      data: data ?? this.data,
    93→      createdAt: createdAt ?? this.createdAt,
    94→      retryCount: retryCount ?? this.retryCount,
    95→      nextRetryAt: nextRetryAt ?? this.nextRetryAt,
    96→      priority: priority ?? this.priority,
    97→      canBatch: canBatch ?? this.canBatch,
    98→      batchId: batchId ?? this.batchId,
    99→      version: version ?? this.version,
   100→    );
   101→  }
   102→

210→          "notes": "Implemented comprehensive reactive state management system (1,990+ lines):

**New State Model Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state.dart (128 lines)
  - SyncState with comprehensive sync state tracking
  - Fields: status, queueSize, isProcessing, lastStatusChangeAt, lastSuccessfulSyncAt, lastSuccessCount, lastFailureCount, lastError, hasPendingOperations
  - Factory constructor: initial()
  - Computed properties: isSyncing, wasLastSyncSuccessful, didLastSyncFail, hasQueue, lastTotalOperations, lastSuccessRate
  - Immutable with copyWith() method supporting all fields plus clearLastError flag
  - Full Equatable implementation for efficient rebuilds

**New Notifier Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier.dart (159 lines)
  - SyncStateNotifier: StateNotifier for comprehensive sync state management
  - Subscribes to syncService.statusStream and syncService.queueStream
  - _initialize(): Sets up subscriptions and loads initial state
  - _onStatusChanged(): Handles status stream events, updates state with timestamps
  - _onQueueChanged(): Handles queue stream events, updates queue size and pending status
  - _updateStateFromService(): Loads current service state during initialization
  - refresh(): Manually refresh state from service
  - reset(): Reset to initial state
  - Comprehensive error handling for both streams
  - Proper disposal of subscriptions

**New Providers Added:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/providers/sync_providers.dart (158 line additions)
  - syncStateNotifierProvider: Main notifier provider
  - syncStateProvider: Current state provider
  - globalSyncStatusProvider: Current sync status (all operations, not just manual)
  - isGloballySyncingProvider: Boolean indicating if ANY sync is running
  - globalQueueSizeProvider: Current queue size
  - hasGlobalPendingOperationsProvider: Boolean for pending operations
  - lastSuccessfulSyncTimeProvider: Timestamp of last successful sync
  - wasLastSyncSuccessfulProvider: Success status of last sync
  - globalSyncStatusTextProvider: User-friendly status text with dynamic messages
  - syncStatusDetailsProvider: Detailed status with timing and success rates

**New Example Widget Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/sync_reactive_status_example.dart (448 lines)
  - SyncReactiveStatusExample: Complete demonstration of reactive updates
  - _AppBarStatusIndicator: Status in app bar that updates reactively
  - _StatusOverviewCard: Main status card with icon and description

  - _QueueInfoCard: Queue size and pending operations display
  - _DetailedStatusCard: Comprehensive status with timestamps and success rates
  - _ActionButtons: Test buttons to verify reactivity
  - Demonstrates all components updating immediately when sync state changes

**Documentation Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/README_REACTIVE_STATUS.md (344 lines)
  - Architecture overview with state flow diagram
  - Key components descriptions (SyncState, SyncStateNotifier, Providers)

  - Computed property tests (isSyncing, wasLastSyncSuccessful, didLastSyncFail, hasQueue, lastTotalOperations, lastSuccessRate)

**Key Features:**
✓ Zero-latency updates from sync state change to UI update (event-driven, no polling)
✓ SyncStateNotifier subscribes to statusStream and queueStream for immediate updates
✓ 10+ providers expose different state aspects for flexible consumption
✓ Multiple components can watch independently and all update simultaneously
✓ Single source of truth (SyncStateNotifier) prevents stale indicators
✓ Immutable state with Equatable ensures efficient rebuilds
✓ Riverpod's watch() triggers immediate UI updates when state changes
